mnemonics.title: FINAL Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: FINAL stands for: F-ixed, I-mmutable, N-ot overrideable, A-llocates once, L-ast forever (in context)

mnemonics.title: Final Class Rule
mnemonics.content: A FINAL CLASS is like a sealed book - no one can EXTEND its story. Think: 'Final = The End!'

mnemonics.title: Final Method Rule
mnemonics.content: FINAL method = 'Locked' method. Like a locked door - you can see it (inherit) but cannot change it (override).

acronyms.usage: Remember the five key properties of the final keyword in Java

visualTips.visualization: Picture a variable as a box with a lock. A final variable is a box that can only be filled ONCE, then the lock is sealed forever.
Think of a final method as a tattoo - once applied (defined in parent), it cannot be changed (overridden) in descendants.
Imagine a final class as the last leaf on a tree - it has no branches (subclasses) coming from it. The family tree ends there.
A static final is like a community bulletin board - it's shared (static) and cannot be changed (final). Everyone sees the same message.

analogies.analogy: Like writing your name in permanent marker on paper - once written, it cannot be erased or changed.
Like a recipe passed down - the original recipe (final method) stays the same, though family members can make other recipes (other methods).
Like the number 1 in mathematics - you cannot create anything 'more first' than 1. It stands alone.
Like a check that is signed but has no amount written yet - the amount (value) must be filled in (initialized) before it can be cashed (used).
